passage: Researchers at a national health organization tracked vaccination rates among children under five in several countries. According to their report, vaccination rates in Country A increased steadily from 2019 to 2023.
stem: Which choice most effectively uses data from the figure to support the researchers' claim?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In Country A, vaccination rates rose from 62% in 2019 to 71% in 2020, then to 79% in 2021, 85% in 2022, and 93% in 2023.

choices.label: B
choices.text: In Country B, vaccination rates were 88% in 2019 and 91% in 2023, indicating a modest increase over the same period.

choices.label: C
choices.text: In Country A, the vaccination rate was 93% in 2023, which was higher than Country B's rate of 91% that same year.

choices.label: D
choices.text: In Country C, vaccination rates declined from 75% in 2019 to 68% in 2023, showing an opposite pattern to other countries.

explanation: The claim states that vaccination rates in Country A increased steadily from 2019 to 2023. Choice A directly supports this by listing every year's rate for Country A, showing a consistent upward trend from 62% to 93% with no reversals. Choice B discusses Country B, not Country A. Choice C only provides a single-year snapshot comparison rather than showing the trend. Choice D discusses Country C's decline.

distractorRationale.A: Correct: cites Country A's vaccination rates for all five years, demonstrating a steady increase from 62% to 93%.
distractorRationale.B: Wrong (irrelevant data): accurately cites Country B data but does not address the claim about Country A.
distractorRationale.C: Wrong (incomplete evidence): cites only the 2023 endpoint for Country A without showing the trend over time, which is what the claim asserts.
distractorRationale.D: Wrong (opposite trend): cites Country C data showing a decline, which is unrelated to the claim about Country A's steady increase.

imageDescription: Line graph titled 'Childhood Vaccination Rates by Country (2019-2023).' X-axis: Year (2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023). Y-axis: Vaccination Rate (0-100%). Three lines: Country A: 62%, 71%, 79%, 85%, 93%. Country B: 88%, 89%, 90%, 90%, 91%. Country C: 75%, 73%, 72%, 70%, 68%. Source: National Health Organization, 2024.

passageMetadata.source: Adapted from National Health Organization global immunization report
